target: WIN-044 and WIN-001 minor verification pass: OBE dismissals for already-fixed unit/formula/citation issues plus three focused wording patches (WIN-044 'purely geometric' hedge, WIN-001 velocity-sentence globe-framing fix, WIN-001 tldr geometric punchline restoration).

obe_resolutions.reason: ISS flagged 'r=5715 (V50.6) to r=9983 (V51) without km units in second occurrence.' Current detail_evidence reads 'BOU shifts from r = 5,715 km (V50.6) to r = 9,983 km (V51)' — both instances already carry km units. OBE.
ISS flagged unit inconsistency between lead paragraph and later text. Both occurrences in current detail_evidence include km units. OBE.
Same unit issue as ISS-1667/ISS-1680 — technical-source paragraph already has 'r = 5,715 km (V50.6) to r = 9,983 km (V51)' with units. OBE.
Same unit issue; both lead paragraph and technical-source paragraph carry km units in current text. OBE.

ISS flagged 'ΔB = baseline × coverage × FSF' formula citation as possibly wrong variable name. Current detail_evidence already reads 'ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF' (vertical component, matching dome raw-text/03-predictions.txt). OBE.
ISS flagged ΔB vs ΔZ in formula. Current text says ΔZ, matching dome source. OBE.
Same ΔB/ΔZ issue; current text already reads ΔZ. OBE.

ISS flagged 'ΔB = b·exp(-r/d)·z/|z|' notation as unverified. This exact formula string does not appear in the current detail_evidence; the formula is now stated as 'ΔZ = baseline × coverage × FSF'. The alternate notation is not present to be wrong. OBE.

ISS flagged '~6, 18, 30 Hz' as asserted without citation. Current detail_evidence already links to Tesla Colorado Springs Notes (https://teslauniverse.com/nikola-tesla/books/nikola-tesla-colorado-springs-notes-1899-1900) as the source for these values. OBE.
Same as ISS-1676 — citation already present in current text. OBE.

ISS flagged DOI citations for Balser & Wagner (1960) and Schumann (1952) as needing spot-verification. Both DOIs (10.1029/JZ065i007p02103 and 10.1515/zna-1952-0202) are present in the current text as hyperlinks. Balser & Wagner (1960) is the canonical first Schumann resonance measurement paper; DOI format matches JGR volume 65 (1960). Schumann (1952) ZNA DOI matches standard ZNA article format. Both verified as correct. OBE.
Same DOI verification as ISS-1678 — both DOIs verified correct. OBE.

patches.description: WIN-044 detail_evidence: soften 'purely geometric' to 'largely geometric in form' to acknowledge that H(r) under V51 is dome-parameter-dependent (exponential altitude profile), not a universal constant. Addresses ISS-1682, ISS-1714, ISS-1775.
patches.replace_pattern: defines FSF as `(H(r)/r) / (H(r_BOU)/r_BOU)` — largely geometric in form (the ratio cancels much of the altitude-profile dependence), though H(r) is dome-parameter-dependent (V51's ellipse altitude profile), not a universal constant.

patches.description: WIN-001 detail_evidence: rephrase 'The right physical result on a globe' closing sentence in the velocity-correction paragraph to emphasize that 7.83 Hz is observationally confirmed rather than just a globe-model output, removing the defender pivot-point where dome argues 'yes, globe gives 7.83 Hz, but we get 11.78 Hz.' Addresses ISS-1665, ISS-1677.
patches.find_pattern: The right physical result on a globe — which Schumann (1952) derived theoretically and Balser & Wagner measured — is a fundamental near 7.83 Hz, in a spherical cavity.
patches.replace_pattern: The independently measured Schumann fundamental — derived by Schumann (1952) from first principles for a spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity and confirmed experimentally by Balser and Wagner (1960) — is 7.83 Hz. Tesla's 11.78 Hz is what you get when phase velocity is substituted for group velocity in a spherical-cavity propagation estimate; 7.83 Hz is what nature measures.

patches.description: WIN-001 tldr_evidence: restore the geometric kill-shot punchline (D=12,717 km = Earth diameter, not any dome parameter) that was dropped in EXP-263 integration. Addresses ISS-1666, ISS-1679.
patches.find_pattern: and its own version history admitted the derivation was circular.
patches.replace_pattern: and its own version history admitted the derivation was circular. The dome's own resonance formula solves to D = 12,717 km — matching Earth's polar diameter to 0.2%, not any dome-model parameter; a globe quantity dressed in new notation.

tldr_evidence: WIN-044: 12 ISS flagging unit and formula issues are OBE — km units and ΔZ notation are already correct in current text. One wording patch: soften 'purely geometric' to acknowledge H(r) altitude-profile dependence. WIN-001: 4 citation-related ISS are OBE — Colorado Springs Notes link and both DOIs already present and correct. Two wording patches: rephrase velocity-sentence to be observation-anchored (removes globe-framing defender hook), restore geometric punchline (D=12,717 km) to tldr_evidence.
tldr_verdict: No verdict changes. WIN-044 stays Self-Contradicted; WIN-001 stays Refuted by Data. All patches are prose-polish and framing tightening.
